preamble: The shift from 'ɡʲ' to 'ɟ' represents the fronting of a voiced velar stop to a palatal position. Like its voiceless counterpart, it is a frequent intermediate stage in the palatalization processes of many language families.

examples.note: Fronting of voiced velars in early stages of palatalization.
Intermediate stage in the palatalization of voiced velars before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
